description: Happy, bright love song with infectious energy and feel-good chorus perfect for sunny days
suno_tags: upbeat pop, bright synths, acoustic guitar, happy love song, catchy melody, female vocals, 118 BPM
vocal_style: Bright, cheerful, and playful with natural warmth. Light and bouncy in verses, joyfully powerful in choruses. Think sunshine in vocal form — smiling while singing.

tips: Write like you're telling your best friend about falling in love
Use everyday moments — grocery store, morning coffee, car rides
Chorus should make people want to dance and sing along
Keep the language bright and colorful — sunshine, golden, sparkling
Pre-chorus should feel like building excitement before a surprise

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]

[Verse 1]
You showed up like a Saturday
No plans, just tangled in the sun
You laughed and spilled your lemonade
And that's when all the good begun

[Pre-Chorus]
Oh, I didn't see it coming
But I'm glad I didn't run

[Chorus]
You're my favorite kind of trouble
Dancing barefoot on the lawn
Every day's a little brighter
Since the day you came along

[Verse 2]
You sing off-key in the kitchen
Burn the toast but make it right
Steal my fries and all my sweaters
And I wouldn't trade a night

[Pre-Chorus]
Oh, it's messy and it's perfect
And it's everything I want

[Chorus]
You're my favorite kind of trouble
Dancing barefoot on the lawn
Every day's a little brighter
Since the day you came along

[Bridge]
I used to think that love was quiet
But you're a firework parade
Loud and colorful and certain
The best choice I ever made

[Chorus]
You're my favorite kind of trouble
Dancing barefoot on the lawn
Every day's a little brighter
Since the day you came along

[Outro]
Since the day you came along
[End]
do: Use specific, playful details that feel real and relatable
Keep the energy consistently uplifting throughout
Make the chorus short, catchy, and instantly singable
Include hand-clap or finger-snap friendly rhythms
Let the bridge add emotional depth without losing the joy
dont: Don't add melancholy or doubt — this is pure happy
Don't use heavy or dark instrumentation
Don't write overly poetic or complex metaphors — keep it breezy
Don't make verses longer than 4 lines — keep the pace moving
Don't forget a strong melodic hook in the chorus first line
